---
title: "Figure S6"
author: "Phil Zhou"
date: "2025-11-18"
output: html_document
---

```{r load_packages}
library(svglite)
library(readr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(readxl)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
library(viridis)
library(gplots)
library(zoo)
library(patchwork)
library(corrplot)
library(ggpubr)
library(tidyr)
library(here)
```

```{r load_dfs}
GOF_mCherry <- read.csv(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "FigS6_GOF mCherry.csv")
)

FigS6_Pitstop2_4h_1h <- read.csv(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "FigS6_Pitstop2 4h_1h.csv")
)

FigS6_Pitstop2_variant_data <- read.csv(
  here("data", "LDLR_domains", "FigS6_Pitstop2 variant data.csv")
)
```
```{r fig_S6A}
# Desired order of constructs
desired_order <- c("wt", "D221Y", "D224H", "S213L", "G219D", "K223E")

# Define fill colors (one per construct)
fill_colors <- c(
  "wt"     = "gray60",
  "D221Y"  = "#DE647D",
  "D224H"  = "#DE647D",
  "S213L"  = "steelblue",
  "G219D"  = "steelblue",
  "K223E"  = "steelblue"
)

comparisons <- list(
  c("wt", "D221Y"),
  c("wt", "D224H"),
  c("wt", "S213L"),
  c("wt", "G219D"),
  c("wt", "K223E")
)

df_long <- GOF_mCherry %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "construct",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(value), is.finite(value))

df_summary <- df_long %>%
  group_by(construct) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem  = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Make sure construct is treated as a factor with that exact order
df_summary$construct <- factor(df_summary$construct, levels = desired_order)
df_long$construct    <- factor(df_long$construct, levels = desired_order)

# Y-axis scaling for stats placement
y_max_bar <- max(df_summary$mean + df_summary$sem, na.rm = TRUE)
y_min_raw <- min(df_long$value, na.rm = TRUE)
y_range   <- y_max_bar - y_min_raw
if (y_range == 0) y_range <- 1

# Holm-corrected Welch t-tests
stat_test_S6A <- compare_means(
  value ~ construct,
  data = df_long,
  method = "t.test",
  method.args = list(var.equal = FALSE),
  ref.group = "wt",
  p.adjust.method = "holm"
) %>%
  filter(group1 == "wt") %>%
  arrange(factor(group2, levels = desired_order)) %>%   # ensures correct order
  mutate(
    p.signif = case_when(
      p.adj <= 1e-4 ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 1e-3 ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 1e-2 ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05 ~ "*",
      TRUE ~ "n.s."
    ),
    y.position = y_max_bar + c(0.08, 0.17, 0.26, 0.35, 0.44) * y_range
  )

# base: use raw data
fig_S6A <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = construct, y = value, fill = construct)) +
  geom_col(
    data = df_summary,
    aes(x = construct, y = mean, fill = construct),
    color = "black",
    width = 0.6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = df_summary,
    aes(x = construct, ymin = mean - sem, ymax = mean + sem),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
    stat_test_S6A,
    label = "p.signif",
    xmin = "group1",
    xmax = "group2",
    y.position = "y.position",
    inherit.aes = FALSE,
    tip.length = 0.01,
    bracket.size = 0.6,
    size = 5,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "MEAN LDLR-mCherry fluorescence\n(LFC vs. LDLR-wt)"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = max(stat_test_S6A$y.position) * 1.05)

#print out figure panel
fig_S6A

stat_test_S6A

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_S6"), "FigS6A.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_S6A,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_S6B}
df_long <- GOF_mCherry %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "construct",
    values_to = "value"
  )

pairwise.t.test(
  x = df_long$value,
  g = df_long$construct,
  pool.sd = FALSE,
  paired = FALSE,
  p.adjust.method = "none"
)

# Desired order of constructs
desired_order <- c("DMSO", "Pitstop2")

# Define fill colors (one per construct)
fill_colors <- c(
  "DMSO"     = "gray60",
  "Pitstop2"  = "#8dfa87"
)

comparisons <- list(
  c("DMSO" , "Pitstop2")
)

df_long <- FigS6_Pitstop2_4h_1h %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "construct",
    values_to = "value"
  )

df_summary <- df_long %>%
  group_by(construct) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem  = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# base: use raw data
fig_S6B <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = construct, y = value, fill = construct)) +
  # bar (use summary df)
  geom_col(
    data = df_summary,
    aes(x = construct, y = mean, fill = construct),
    color = "black",
    width = 0.6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # error bars (also from summary)
  geom_errorbar(
    data = df_summary,
    aes(x = construct, ymin = mean - sem, ymax = mean + sem),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  # jitter (from long, so inherits)
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  # your colors
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  # stats (now the plot’s main data has y = value, so this is happy)
  stat_compare_means(
    comparisons = comparisons,
    method = "t.test",
    label = "p.signif", symnum.args = list(cutpoints = c(0, 0.0001, 0.001, 0.01, 0.05, Inf), symbols = c("****", "***", "**", "*", "n.s."))
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
  x = NULL,
  y = "Change in LDL uptake\n(4 hr/1 hr treatment LFC)"
) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = max(df_summary$mean + df_summary$sem) * 1.2)

#print out figure panel
fig_S6B

pairwise.t.test(
  x = df_long$value,
  g = df_long$construct,
  pool.sd = FALSE,
  paired = FALSE,
  p.adjust.method = "none"
)

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_S6"), "FigS6B.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_S6B,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 4,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r fig_S6C}
# Desired order of constructs
desired_order <- c("wt", "D221Y", "D224H", "S213L", "G219D", "K223E")

# Define fill colors
fill_colors <- c(
  "wt"     = "gray60",
  "D221Y"  = "#DE647D",
  "D224H"  = "#DE647D",
  "S213L"  = "steelblue",
  "G219D"  = "steelblue",
  "K223E"  = "steelblue"
)

comparisons <- list(
  c("wt", "D221Y"),
  c("wt", "D224H"),
  c("wt", "S213L"),
  c("wt", "G219D"),
  c("wt", "K223E")
)

df_long <- FigS6_Pitstop2_variant_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = everything(),
    names_to = "construct",
    values_to = "value"
  ) %>%
  mutate(construct = factor(construct, levels = desired_order))

df_summary <- df_long %>%
  group_by(construct) %>%
  summarise(
    n    = sum(!is.na(value)),
    mean = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd   = sd(value, na.rm = TRUE),
    sem  = sd / sqrt(n),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(construct = factor(construct, levels = desired_order))

# ---- 1) Run only the 5 desired Welch t-tests ----
stat_test <- map_dfr(comparisons, \(comp) {
  g1 <- comp[1]
  g2 <- comp[2]

  x1 <- df_long %>% filter(construct == g1) %>% pull(value)
  x2 <- df_long %>% filter(construct == g2) %>% pull(value)

  tt <- t.test(x1, x2, var.equal = FALSE)

  tibble(
    group1 = g1,
    group2 = g2,
    p = tt$p.value
  )
})

# ---- 2) Holm correction across those 5 tests ---- 
stat_test <- stat_test %>%
  mutate(
    p.adj = p.adjust(p, method = "holm"),
    p.signif = case_when(
      p.adj <= 0.0001 ~ "****",
      p.adj <= 0.001  ~ "***",
      p.adj <= 0.01   ~ "**",
      p.adj <= 0.05   ~ "*",
      TRUE            ~ "n.s."
    )
  )

stat_test

# ---- 3) Add y positions for annotation ----

y_base  <- max(df_summary$mean + df_summary$sem, na.rm = TRUE)
y_range <- diff(range(df_long$value, na.rm = TRUE))

stat_test <- stat_test %>%
  mutate(
    y.position = y_base + c(0.08, 0.16, 0.24, 0.32, 0.40) * y_range
  )

# ---- 4) Plot using Holm-adjusted significance labels ----

fig_S6C <- ggplot(df_long, aes(x = construct, y = value, fill = construct)) +
  geom_col(
    data = df_summary,
    aes(x = construct, y = mean, fill = construct),
    color = "black",
    width = 0.6,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    data = df_summary,
    aes(x = construct, ymin = mean - sem, ymax = mean + sem),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.4,
    inherit.aes = FALSE
  ) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.15, size = 1.6, alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = fill_colors) +
  stat_pvalue_manual(
  stat_test,
  label = "p.signif",
  xmin = "group1",
  xmax = "group2",
  y.position = "y.position",
  tip.length = 0.01,
  inherit.aes = FALSE
) +
  theme_classic() +
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = "Mean BODIPY-LDL Delta LFC"
  ) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.text.y  = element_text(size = 18, color = "black"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 20, color = "black"),
    legend.position = "none"
  ) +
  expand_limits(y = max(stat_test$y.position) + 0.05 * y_range)

#print out figure panel
fig_S6C

#save figure panel
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_S6"), "FigS6C.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_S6C,
  width    = 6,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```

```{r printing_fig_S6}
fig_S6 <- fig_S6A + fig_S6B + fig_S6C
fig_S6

ggsave(
  filename = file.path(here("output", "figure_S6"), "Fig_S6.pdf"),
  plot     = fig_S6,
  width    = 10,
  height   = 6,
  units    = "in",
  dpi = 600
)
```
